title: rubus obtruncatus subsp. pilocarpus
intro: Rubus obtruncatus subsp. pilocarpus, also known as the Himalayan raspberry or Himalayan bramble, is a subspecies of the Rubus obtruncatus plant, a member of the rose family (Rosaceae). It is a climbing shrub native to the Himalayas and the Indian subcontinent, known for its medicinal properties, particularly as a diuretic and anti-inflammatory agent. The plant's leaves and roots have been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, digestive issues, and skin problems, and its health benefits include reducing inflammation, improving circulation, and supporting urinary health. Rubus obtruncatus subsp. pilocarpus can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, and its extracts have also been used in traditional medicine to create remedies for various ailments.
